title: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: October 7: New report documents extent of sexual violence committed by Hamas

Seine murders: Psychiatric assessment finds suspect cannot be held criminally responsible
subhead: Monji H., 28, is accused of murdering four people whose bodies were discovered in the Seine in August 2025. According to an expert, he cannot be held criminally responsible. A second opinion is expected to be requested.

title: Hantavirus: How great is the transmission risk, as each country sets its own quarantine rules?
subhead: All passengers from the MV Hondius have now been repatriated to their countries of origin. This marks a critical phase, as follow-up and quarantine protocols are not standardized across borders.

title: Scientists warn 2026 could be extreme year as global warming slips down agenda
subhead: Experts and international agencies issued a fresh warning on Tuesday as the anticipated return of the natural El Niño phenomenon is expected to worsen an already accelerating climate crisis.

title: Eye Haïdara, master of ceremonies at the Cannes Film Festival: 'When you're on stage, you're in charge! You're untouchable'
subhead: The actress, who rose to fame in France in 2017, paid tribute to her primary school teacher, who inspired her passion for theater.

After a decade of crises between France and its former colonies, Macron chooses Kenya for the last major African trip of his presidency
subhead: Coming to power having promised to rebuild the relationship between France and its former colonies, Emmanuel Macron had to face a series of crises, particularly in the Sahel region. In the English-speaking part of the continent, Paris struggled to find its place.

title: Who is Iyad Ag Ghali, the jihadist behind the destabilization of Mali?
subhead: A central figure in Sahelian jihad for nearly two decades, pursued in turn by the French and the Russians, Ghali led the offensive against the junta in Bamako. Since 2012, the jihadist and leader of Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) has softened his rhetoric, seeking to appear more approachable and less radical.

title: Inside the mutually beneficial relationship of shipping magnate Saadé and President Macron
subhead: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (1/4) The French leader and the head of the company CMA CGM have developed a relationship that is as respectful as it is useful for both parties, given how closely the interests of the family-owned group and the French state are intertwined.
